atuacao.heading:
From the origin

to key handover.
atuacao.intro: Open Group works from the first reading of the opportunity through handover, structuring each stage so product, capital, partners, and execution move together.

modelos.heading:
Flexible engagement based on

the profile of the opportunity.
modelos.intro: Each opportunity calls for its own logic. Open Group assesses the stage, the potential, and the complexity of the venture to define the most suitable engagement model.

consultoria.title: Contracted consulting
consultoria.desc: We support the structuring of the venture with strategic reading and technical guidance, within a defined scope.

parceria.title: Strategic partnership
parceria.desc: We work side by side on building the development, sharing vision, structure, and direction.

societaria.title: Equity participation
societaria.desc: We come in as partners in the venture, committed to the result of the development from end to end.

investimento.title: Investment SCP
investimento.desc: Direct capital in the venture through an SCP (silent partnership) — capital that shares in the result, without taking on management.

manifesto.line2: Before the launch, there is the structure.
manifesto.body1: Open Group Real Estate Development works on the structuring and development of real estate ventures, guiding opportunities from concept to launch.
manifesto.body2: Our work begins before construction: in reading the opportunity, in feasibility analysis, in the strategic design of the venture, and in bringing together the partners needed for the project to move forward with consistency.
manifesto.body3: We work alongside builders, developers, companies holding real estate assets, and investors, combining strategic vision, financial and institutional structuring, connection with partners, and guidance through the critical market stages.

rail.ariaLabel: Visual navigation of the About page

meta.title: What we do
meta.description: Open Group structures and develops real estate ventures, guiding opportunities from concept to launch. Value is born before ground is broken.

about.heading: Our work begins before ground is broken.
about.body1: Open Group Real Estate Development works on the structuring and development of real estate ventures, guiding opportunities from concept to launch.
about.body2: Our work begins before construction: in reading the opportunity, in feasibility analysis, in the strategic design of the venture, and in bringing together the partners needed for a consistent launch.
about.body3: We work alongside builders, developers, landowners, companies holding real estate assets, and investors — combining strategic vision, financial and institutional structuring, connection with partners, and guidance through the decisive market stages.
about.body4: More than developing projects, we structure real estate ventures so that potential, capital, and execution move in the same direction — from the start to the handover of keys.

manifesto.thesis: Many real estate opportunities don't fail for lack of potential. They fail because they move forward without structure and because they lack vision.
manifesto.elaboration: A promising site, a well-located project, or a market opportunity do not hold up on their own. Without strategic vision, clear feasibility, the right partners, and precise guidance, potential dissipates before it becomes a development.
manifesto.bridgeLabel: Value is born before the works
manifesto.bridge: Open Group believes that a development's value is born before the works.
manifesto.positioning: That is why we do not treat real estate development as isolated execution. We treat it as the strategic construction of value.
manifesto.institutional: We structure and develop real estate projects end to end, connecting vision, feasibility, partners, and operation to turn opportunities into projects ready to move forward.
manifesto.closing: Because developing well means deciding better from the origin.

01.name: In the right reading of the opportunity
01.desc: Interpreting the asset and its real potential, before it becomes obvious.

02.name: In the conception of the product
02.desc: Defining the right development for that opportunity.

03.name: In the structuring of the venture
03.desc: Aligning potential, capital, and execution in the same direction.

04.name: In the choice of partners
04.desc: Bringing together partners, the bank, and institutional relationships.

05.name: In the clarity of the path to launch
05.desc: Guiding with clarity until the project is ready to move forward.

lideranca.heading: The leadership behind every structured venture.
lideranca.intro: Open Group is led by professionals who combine strategic vision, command of the real estate venture, and institutional relationships — to turn opportunities into developments ready to move forward.

max.statement: Before developing a project, you must see the value that hasn't yet been revealed.
max.quote1: At the helm of Open Group, Flying Studio, NID Studio, and Rinno Films, I lead an ecosystem created to turn real estate opportunities into clearer, more feasible ventures, ready to move forward.
max.quote2: Each company works on one stage of the journey, from strategy to communication, always with the same vision: to develop with structure from the very origin.

grupo.heading: Our Group
grupo.intro: Strategy, architecture, technology, and audiovisual work as complementary forces. Each company deepens one stage of the journey: to structure, to design, to anticipate, and to communicate.

flying.text: The machine for anticipating futures. Images and 3D renders, apps, D.Brave, and immersive rooms transport the client into their future reality — over 12 years translating brick and concrete into pure visual poetry.

ogdi.text: The strategic starting point. We provide consulting focused on market intelligence, product development, and the complete structuring of new launches from their very conception.

nid.role: Architecture, interiors, and point of sale
nid.text: Where architectural intelligence takes shape and emotion. The home as a true nest: interior projects, facade design, and points of sale conceived as spaces of comfort, security, and strong personality.

rinno.text: The group's audiovisual studio. From concept and product films to viral and institutional pieces — bringing motion to stories and creating the emotional connection between the project and its audience.

perguntas.heading:
Investing before construction,

no fine print.
perguntas.asideText: Didn't find your question? <link>Talk to the team</link>.

01.q: What is an SCP, and what are the roles of the managing and silent partner?
01.a: In a Sociedade em Conta de Participação (a Brazilian silent partnership), the managing partner — usually the developer — runs the venture and is the only one who appears before suppliers, the bank, and clients. The silent partner (the investor) contributes capital and shares the result in proportion to their stake, without managing or signing contracts. The SCP has no legal personality: it exists internally, between the partners, governed by contract.

02.q: How far does my risk go as a silent partner?
02.a: As a rule, your liability is limited to the amount you contribute — you don't answer to suppliers, the bank, or clients. But article 993 of the Civil Code is clear: if the silent partner interferes in external management (signs contracts, negotiates with third parties), they become jointly liable. The protection is real, as long as the investor stays out of the venture's administration.

03.q: How are the profits I receive from an SCP taxed?
03.a: The SCP is treated as a legal entity by the tax authority: corporate taxes (IRPJ, CSLL, PIS, COFINS) are assessed and paid by the managing partner. Profits distributed to the silent partner are usually income-tax exempt at the investor's end — but only when there is formally booked accounting profit; an unsupported distribution can be reclassified as taxable compensation. SCPs in development may also use the RET regime (a unified 4% rate). Always confirm with a tax advisor.

04.q: Does the SCP need a tax ID? And how do I receive the return?
04.a: Yes — even without legal personality, the SCP must register a CNPJ (tax ID), tied to the managing partner, who handles the ancillary obligations. The return follows the contract: it can be distributed during sales or at closing, in cash or in units. The milestones and the form of distribution are defined before capital enters.

05.q: Do I pay tax when I swap my land for units (a physical swap)?
05.a: In a pure physical swap — units for units only, by public deed and with no cash involved — an individual books no capital gain; this is the traditionally exempt case. The tax authority, however, reads that exemption narrowly: swapping a residential property for future commercial units, for instance, was deemed taxable (Cosit 128/2024). The treatment depends on what the deed actually says.

06.q: What if there is "torna" (a cash top-up)?
06.a: If there is torna, tax falls only on the cash portion, at capital-gains rates of 15% to 22.5%. The part actually swapped for property remains untaxed. In other words, it's the cash top-up that's taxed, not the swap itself.

07.q: What is a "financial swap" and why did it change in 2025?
07.a: It's when the landowner, instead of receiving units, becomes entitled to a percentage of sales revenue — cash only. Tax ruling Cosit nº 89/2025 determined this is not a swap but a deferred sale, fully subject to capital gains (15% to 22.5%) for individuals. Anyone who structured this arrangement expecting it to be exempt was caught off guard — hence the importance of an up-to-date opinion.

08.q: When do I pay the tax on a swap?
08.a: For units yet to be built, capital-gains tax is paid by the last business day of the month following receipt of each unit — not on the date of the deed. In a financial swap, it follows the receipt of each cash installment. For a developer under the RET regime, taxation stays unified at 4% of revenue.

09.a: It means pooling several investors' capital to take part in the same development, sharing the contribution, the risk, and the result. You don't hold a specific property, but a stake in the project. To be clear: "co-investment" describes the logic of investing together — the actual legal vehicle is usually an SCP, an SPE (project company) with shares, or a listed real estate fund (FII).

10.q: What's the difference between co-investing (via SCP/SPE) and a listed real estate fund (FII)?
10.a: An FII is regulated by the securities commission, has exchange-traded shares, a professional manager, and high liquidity — but the share price swings like any equity. Co-investment via SCP/SPE is private, illiquid, and tied to a single project with few investors — higher concentrated-return potential, in exchange for a difficult exit and less regulation. It's the trade-off between liquidity and regulation on one side and concentrated return on the other.

11.q: How do I receive the return in a co-investment?
11.a: Per the vehicle's contract: distribution of profits during sales or at the project's closing, in cash or in units. Unlike an FII, there is no guaranteed periodic distribution and no tradable share — the return is tied to the performance of that specific development.

12.q: What are the main risks of private co-investment?
12.a: Illiquidity (it's hard to exit before the end), dependence on the competence and integrity of whoever structures it, project-execution risk, and less transparency than a regulated vehicle. No structure eliminates risk — which is why assessing who runs the venture matters as much as assessing the property.

13.q: Why is it said that value is created before construction?
13.a: Because it's during structuring — approvals, design, making the land viable, and opening sales — that the development gains value, before construction reflects it in the price. Those who enter early usually access terms below the future price table, taking on part of the development risk in return. It's performance-based return, not fixed income.

14.q: What are the main risks of entering before construction?
14.a: Construction delays, slow sales, buyer cancellations, and — most seriously in the recent cycle — the developer's financial health: in 2024 the sector led judicial-recovery filings. Any promise of fixed, guaranteed return should raise a flag. "Before construction" is not a guaranteed gain.

15.q: Is there liquidity? Can I exit before the end?
15.a: Liquidity is low. Unlike an FII with exchange-traded shares, exiting an SCP or a co-investment depends on a contract clause and on someone to take over your position. It's best to plan for capital committed until the project concludes — generally a medium- to long-term horizon (2 to 4 years or more).

16.q: What protects me if the developer runs into trouble?
16.a: The main instrument is the patrimônio de afetação (ring-fencing, Law 10.931/2004): it segregates the development's land, construction, and receivables from the rest of the developer. In a bankruptcy, those assets stay out of the insolvency estate and the project can be completed. Checking whether the specific development has ring-fencing in place is a critical due-diligence item.

17.q: What guarantees can I have in the venture?
17.a: Beyond ring-fencing, the contract may provide collateral (a mortgage or fiduciary lien on the land), guarantees from the partners, fiduciary assignment of receivables, and preference clauses. None of them eliminates project-execution risk, but they all reduce the risk of total loss.

18.q: What should I check in due diligence before investing?
18.a: The land's title record and chain of ownership (liens, mortgages, encumbrances), zoning and permits, the development's registration filing, whether ring-fencing is in place, and — the most underestimated point — the financial health and integrity of the developer and its partners. Today the practice spans legal, engineering, and accounting review. Analyze the company, not just the property.

19.q: How do I track the venture and its governance?
19.a: Through periodic reporting on construction and sales progress (sold vs. budgeted), the vehicle's financial statements, and movement in the project's segregated account. In an SCP, the silent partner has the right to oversee but not to manage — and that right should be stated in the contract. A lack of transparency is, in itself, a warning sign.

20.q: What's the horizon, and how is the return taxed at my end?
20.a: The horizon follows the development's cycle — generally 2 to 4 years or more, until construction is finished and sales are settled; there is no guaranteed fixed date. Taxation depends on the structure: SCP profits tend to be exempt at your end (where there is booked profit), an FII has its own exemption for individuals, and a swap/sale follows capital gains. Always confirm with a tax advisor before investing.

meta.title: Privacy Policy
meta.description: How Open Group collects, uses, stores, and protects personal data, in compliance with Brazil's data protection law, the LGPD (Law No. 13,709/2018).

privacidade.title: Privacy Policy
privacidade.intro: Privacy and the protection of personal data are part of Open Group's commitment to responsible guidance. This document explains, transparently, how we handle the data of those who browse this site and of those who engage with our team.

s1.heading: Purpose and scope
paras: This Privacy Policy describes how Open Group — Real Estate Development (“Open Group”) collects, uses, stores, shares, and protects the personal data of visitors to this site and of people who contact the team, in compliance with Brazil's data protection law, the General Personal Data Protection Law — LGPD (Law No. 13,709/2018).

It applies exclusively to this institutional site and to the contact channels made available here. Any third-party sites that may be linked (such as partner pages and map platforms) have their own policies, which we recommend consulting.

s2.heading: Data we collect
paras: We process two categories of personal data:
list: Data you provide — when you use the contact form or the direct channels (email and phone), we collect the information you choose to share: name, company or fund, email, WhatsApp/phone, type of contact, and the content of the message, which may include information about assets, land, or projects.
Data collected automatically — technical browsing information, such as IP address, browser type, device, pages visited, and time spent, collected through cookies and similar technologies.
parasAfter: On this site we do not collect sensitive personal data (such as health data, biometrics, or convictions), and our services are not directed to persons under 18 years of age.

s3.heading: Purposes and legal bases
paras: We use personal data for the following purposes, always supported by a legal basis under the LGPD:
list: To respond to your contact and carry out the first reading of the opportunity presented — legal basis: preliminary procedures prior to a contract (art. 7, V) and legitimate interest (art. 7, IX);
To maintain the institutional and commercial relationship arising from the contact — legal basis: legitimate interest;
To comply with legal or regulatory obligations and to exercise rights in proceedings — legal basis: compliance with a legal obligation (art. 7, II) and the regular exercise of rights (art. 7, VI);
To analyze the use of the site and improve the browsing experience — legal basis: consent (art. 7, I) and legitimate interest.

s4.heading: Cookies and browsing technologies
paras: Cookies are small files stored in your browser. We use cookies strictly necessary for the functioning of the site and, where applicable, analytical cookies to understand how the site is used.
You can manage or disable cookies in your browser settings. Disabling necessary cookies may affect the functioning of parts of the site.

s5.heading: Data sharing
paras: Open Group does not sell personal data. Sharing occurs only in the following cases:
list: With service providers that operate the site and the communication channels (hosting, email, analytics tools), limited to what is necessary to provide the service;
With partners in the real estate venture, when the purpose of your contact is an opportunity that requires such coordination — always within the context of the original purpose;
With public authorities, by virtue of a legal or regulatory obligation or a court order;
In corporate transactions (such as a merger or acquisition), in which case the data subject will be informed of the change of controller.

s6.heading: Storage and retention period
paras: Data is stored in controlled environments and may be hosted on servers located outside Brazil — in which case the international transfer observes the provisions of arts. 33 et seq. of the LGPD.

We retain the data for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes of this policy: contact data is retained for the duration of the relationship or the dealing, and thereafter for the period necessary to comply with legal obligations and to exercise rights regularly. Once these periods end, the data is deleted or anonymized.

s7.heading: Information security
paras: We adopt reasonable technical and administrative measures to protect personal data against unauthorized access, loss, alteration, or improper disclosure — including encryption in transit (HTTPS), access control, and minimization of collection.

No system is absolutely secure. Should we identify a security incident that may entail relevant risk or harm to data subjects, we will notify those affected and the National Data Protection Authority (ANPD), as provided by law.

s8.heading: Data subject rights
paras: Under art. 18 of the LGPD, you may request, at any time:
list: Confirmation that your data is being processed;
Access to the data we hold about you;
Correction of incomplete, inaccurate, or outdated data;
Anonymization, blocking, or deletion of data that is unnecessary, excessive, or processed in non-compliance with the law;
Portability of the data to another provider, subject to the regulations of the ANPD;
Deletion of data processed on the basis of your consent;
Information about the entities with which we share your data;
Withdrawal of consent, where this is the legal basis for the processing.
parasAfter: Requests are free of charge and will be answered within the periods provided by law. To exercise them, use the channel indicated in the following section.

s9.heading: Data protection officer and contact channel
paras: Requests relating to personal data should be directed to Open Group's data protection officer at the email {email}, with the subject “Data privacy”. If you prefer, use the contact page of this site.

s10.heading: Applicable law
paras: This policy is governed by the laws of the Federative Republic of Brazil, in particular Law No. 13,709/2018 (LGPD) and the Brazilian Civil Rights Framework for the Internet (Law No. 12,965/2014). The courts of the judicial district of São Paulo/SP are elected to settle any disputes.

s11.heading: Updates to this policy
paras: This policy may be updated at any time to reflect legal, regulatory, or operational changes. The current version will always be available on this page, with the date of update indicated at the top. Relevant changes may be communicated through the contact channels provided.

contact.text: Questions about this policy? Write to <email></email> or visit the <link>contact page</link>.
